race.name: Birkebeinerrennet
race.tagline: Carry the weight of history — 54km with a 3.5kg pack across the Norwegian mountains, just as the Birkebeiner warriors did 800 years ago.

vitals.registration: Opens in spring; sells out quickly
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: March conditions vary from deep cold to spring warmth. The course crosses exposed mountain terrain above treeline where wind chill is severe. Temperatures typically -15°C to 0°C with significant wind on the high plateaus.
challenges: Above-treeline exposure with severe wind chill
High altitude sections can be icy or windswept

course.primary: Point-to-point mountain crossing from Rena to Lillehammer with significant climbing. The course reaches 900m+ altitude on the Birkebeiner ridge before descending into Lillehammer. Demanding elevation profile with the infamous climb to the high point.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through mountain and forest terrain
course.grooming: World-class, Worldloppet standard grooming across the full course
features: Mandatory 3.5kg backpack — unique among Worldloppet races
Crosses the mountain ridge where Birkebeiner warriors carried infant prince Haakon in 1206
Finish in Lillehammer at the 1994 Olympic venue
Exposed above-treeline section with dramatic mountain views
Historical reenactment of one of Norway's most important events

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The mandatory backpack, mountain terrain, and deep historical significance make this one of the most iconic ski marathons in the world. Highest overall score in the database.

history.summary: The race commemorates the 1206 rescue of the infant Prince Haakon Haakonsson by two Birkebeiner warriors who skied him across the mountains from Lillehammer to safety in Rena during the Norwegian civil war. The 3.5kg backpack requirement represents the weight of the child. Founded in 1932, it has become Norway's most celebrated ski marathon and a cornerstone of the Worldloppet series.
notable_facts: Commemorates the 1206 rescue of infant Prince Haakon — the backpack weight represents the baby
The Birkebeiner warriors' name means 'birch legs' from the birch-bark leggings they wore
Finishes at the Lillehammer Olympic cross-country stadium from the 1994 Games
Has inspired the American Birkebeiner (founded 1973) and the Canadian Birkebeiner
Part of both Worldloppet and Ski Classics series
The race has been cancelled multiple times due to extreme weather on the mountain
